pyrann.processing.processing.plot

processing.plot(mapper, color_data, hover_info, color_type='categorical', rescale_colorbar=False, cmap='Viridis256', subset_points=None, key_str=None)

Saves an interactive bokeh html plot to visualize the feature-space.

Parameters:

  • mapper – UMAP instance

  • color_data (ArrayLike) – The data that determines how each data point in the interactive plot is colored.

  • hover_info (dict) – Dictionary containing the information that will appear when a data point is hovered over in the interactive plot. The key will be the label and the values should be arrays of length natoms.

  • color_type (str | None (default: 'categorical')) – Type of coloring to use. If categorical, each unique item in color_data gets its own color. If linear, viridis colormap will be used to color the data from min(color_data) to max(color_data). Defaults to categorical.

  • rescale_colorbar (bool | None (default: False)) – Boolean to rescale colorbar when changing the visible range on the plot if color_type == ‘linear’. Ignored if color_type == ‘categorical’.

  • cmap (str | None (default: 'Viridis256')) – String representation of desiried 256 color palette from Bokeh.

  • subset_points (ArrayLike | None (default: None)) – 1-D array of booleans of length natoms. True means that the data point for that atom should be plotted.

  • key_str (str | None (default: None)) – The string to name the html file.

Notes

Todo

  • Change method to where rescale_colorbar, cmap are kwargs.

  • Add in kwargs for plot size and background fill color.
